problem_statement.title: Only reset token bucket if needed
problem_statement.issue_title: req_throttle does not throttle requests even when no tokens are available
problem_statement.issue_body:
Hi,

I am not sure if I am doing something wrong or if I just don't understand how `req_throttle()` works. But my expectation was that there would only be 1 request per minute based on this configuration. But what happens is that requests are made as fast as possible until the rate limit is hit, even when no tokens are available via `throttle_status()`. Is this a bug or am I doing something wrong?

``` r
library(httr2)

foo <- function() {
  x <-
    request("https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils") |>
    req_url_path_append("esearch.fcgi") |>
    req_url_query(
      db = 'pubmed',
      term = 'Richard Feynman[au]',
      retmode = 'json'
    ) |>
    req_throttle(capacity = 1, fill_time_s = 60) |>
    req_perform() |>
    resp_body_json()

  print(throttle_status())

  x
}

replicate(100, foo())
#>                     realm tokens to_wait
#> 1 eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov      0      60
#>                     realm tokens to_wait
#> 1 eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov      0      59
#>                     realm tokens to_wait
#> 1 eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov      0      60
#> Error in `req_perform()`:
#> ! HTTP 429 Too Many Requests.
```

test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/testthat/test-req-throttle.R b/tests/testthat/test-req-throttle.R
index 3fe80753..ba8316f6 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/test-req-throttle.R
+++ b/tests/testthat/test-req-throttle.R
@@ -27,6 +27,32 @@ test_that("first request isn't throttled", {
   expect_equal(throttle_delay(req), 0.5)
 })
 
+test_that("throttle only reset if parameters change", {
+  on.exit(throttle_reset())
+
+  mock_time <- 0
+  local_mocked_bindings(unix_time = function() mock_time)
+
+  req <- request_test() |> req_throttle(capacity = 1, fill_time_s = 1)
+  expect_equal(throttle_delay(req), 0)
+
+  mock_time <- 0.5
+  expect_equal(throttle_delay(req), 0.5)
+  mock_time <- 1
+
+  req <- request_test() |> req_throttle(capacity = 1, fill_time_s = 1)
+  expect_equal(throttle_delay(req), 1)
+
+  # reset: capacity changed
+  req <- request_test() |> req_throttle(capacity = 2, fill_time_s = 1)
+  expect_equal(throttle_delay(req), 0)
+
+  # reset: fill time changed
+  req <- request_test() |> req_throttle(capacity = 2, fill_time_s = 2)
+  expect_equal(throttle_delay(req), 0)
+})
+
+
 test_that("realm defaults to hostname but can be overridden", {
   on.exit(throttle_reset())
 
@@ -46,7 +72,7 @@ test_that("token bucket respects capacity limits", {
   mock_time <- 0
   local_mocked_bindings(unix_time = function() mock_time)
 
-  bucket <- TokenBucket$new(capacity = 2, fill_time_s = 1)
+  bucket <- TokenBucket$new(capacity = 2, fill_rate = 2)
   expect_equal(bucket$take_token(), 0)
   expect_equal(bucket$tokens, 1)
   expect_equal(bucket$take_token(), 0)
@@ -61,7 +87,7 @@ test_that("token bucket handles fractions correctly", {
   mock_time <- 0
   local_mocked_bindings(unix_time = function() mock_time)
 
-  bucket <- TokenBucket$new(capacity = 2, fill_time_s = 1)
+  bucket <- TokenBucket$new(capacity = 2, fill_rate = 2)
   bucket$tokens <- 0
   expect_equal(bucket$take_token(), 0.5)
   expect_equal(bucket$tokens, -1)

fail_to_pass: throttle only reset if parameters change - verifies that calling req_throttle() with same params preserves token state
throttle_delay returns correct value after multiple req_throttle calls with same params
pass_to_pass: realm defaults to hostname but can be overridden

key_changes: Add a throttle_exists() helper function that checks if a throttle exists for a realm with matching capacity and fill_rate
Modify req_throttle() to only create a new TokenBucket if throttle_exists() returns FALSE
Change TokenBucket$initialize() to accept fill_rate directly instead of fill_time_s
Calculate rate from capacity/fill_time_s when capacity is explicitly provided
potential_pitfalls: Must check both capacity AND fill_rate when determining if throttle exists - checking only one is insufficient
When capacity is provided (not rate), need to calculate rate = capacity / fill_time_s before checking throttle_exists
The TokenBucket constructor signature change requires updating existing tests that instantiate TokenBucket directly
Must use env_has() to check if realm exists in the$throttle before accessing it

instruction: Fix the bug in req_throttle() where the token bucket is reset on every call, preventing proper request throttling. When req_throttle() is called multiple times with the same parameters (same realm, capacity, and fill rate), it should NOT reset the existing token bucket - it should reuse the existing one. The token bucket should only be reset when the throttle parameters (capacity or fill_rate) actually change.
context:
The req_throttle() function uses a TokenBucket R6 class stored in the$throttle environment keyed by realm. The TokenBucket class has fields: capacity, fill_rate, last_fill, and tokens. The bug is that every call to req_throttle() creates a new TokenBucket, resetting the token count and last_fill time, which defeats the purpose of throttling.

Key function signature:
req_throttle <- function(req, rate, capacity, fill_time_s = 60, realm = NULL)

TokenBucket is an R6 class with initialize(capacity, fill_rate), take_token(), and refill() methods.
